title: Calcium Imaging Protocol for hIPSC-derived Dopaminergic neurons
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"75gmv","text":"This is the imaging protocol to analyse calcium dynamics in vitro KOLF2.1J differentiated midbrain dopaminergic neurons","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":57,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the dye-loading solution in Krebs-Ringer buffer: 5 µM Fluo-4 AM Fluo-4 Calcium Imaging Kit Thermo Fisher Catalog #F10489 0.625% Pluronic F-127 Pluronic® F-127 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P2443 Mix thoroughly to ensure dye solubilization.
Add loading solution to the 35 mm culture dishes containing live cells.
Incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes in the dark . 37 °C
After incubation, wash cells twice with fresh Krebs-Ringer buffer to remove excess dye.
Maintain cells in fresh Krebs-Ringer buffer at 37°C during imaging. 37 °C

Perform imaging using a Nikon CrEST X-Light V3 spinning disk confocal microscope : Objective: 20x Air lens Excitation wavelength: 480 nm Sampling frequency: 1 Hz (one frame per second) Use Nikon NIS-Elements software to control the microscope and acquire time-lapse fluorescence data.

Analyze calcium signals using the following tools: FIJI (ImageJ) for image preprocessing and intensity extraction. ImageJ/Fiji 1.8.0 Windows 7 http://wsr.imagej.net/distros/win/ij152-win-java8.zip National Institutes of Health MATLAB (R2020b) for custom analysis scripts. MATLAB 2017a Microsoft Windows 10 Company Website Mathworks FluoroSNNAP (GitHub: https://github.com/tapan-patel/FluoroSNNAP) for spike detection and activity analysis.
creator: Roberto Garcia Swinburn
